title: Capesize Currents - Rising to a High (What's Next?)
section_path: Capesize Currents - Rising to a High (What's Next?)
section_path_text: Capesize Currents - Rising to a High (What's Next?)
summary:
Main
Image reference: RGB_COLOR_BRS_SHIPBROKERS_MONOGRAM_LOGO.png | assets/2026-05-06_capesize-currents-rising-to-a-high-whats-next_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_c365f35f1de5.png
All said and done,The C5TC (basis 180,000 DwtK) averages for this month is currentlynow staring at around $30,000/day, making this month the strongest April since 2021 ($29,798/day). The difference between C5TC April 2025 and April 2026 is a whopping $14,000/day, an affirmation ofn how Capesize dynamics have structurally morphed. Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-05-05 110633.png | assets/2026-05-06_capesize-currents-rising-to-a-high-whats-next_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_53fd14ba1cee.png
Stripping out bunker-related distortions from the Iran conflict, there is little evidence of a material deterioration in underlying Capesize fundamentals in 1Q26.
